title: Use Template Folder with Dynamic Name
excerpt:

posts.author: tquerruel (Todd)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello all

I’m in the process of creating a fairly simple project management / change approvals system within Monday. To achieve this, I’ve created a parent project listing board and have a “create project” status, which will be locked to only be modifiable by project manager

I’ve been able to automate it so that when a PM approves a project, it creates a board based on a pre-created template and names it with the Pulse Name

I’d like to take this a step further though. Realistically every project will have three boards

  • dashboard
  • project items
  • issues register

I’d like to put them into a sub-folder, and then have the folder itself take on the name of the Pulse item

Does anyone know if this is possible?

posts.author: JennyA (Jenny Anderson)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi there,

Did you manage to figure out how to do this? I’m looking to do the same and it would be helpful to know if you figured out how to do this?

posts.author: brandon1 (Brandon Sprecher)
posts.cookedHtml: Hello! I’m working on building an item view app that would allow users to create folder templates and deploy them using the item names and other item data to name the folders / boards. Right now the issue is that their is no API endpoint for creating / editing folders. As soon as this endpoint is release I will have the app publicly available. Hoping this happens soon

posts.author: brandon1 (Brandon Sprecher)
posts.cookedHtml:
I just submitted an idea requesting the API endpoint, go and upvote this idea to help Monday realize the urgency. You can view the Idea request here:

Folder API Endpoint

posts.author: brandon1 (Brandon Sprecher)
posts.cookedHtml:
